title: Extraction chromatographic studies of aluminium(III) with n-capric acid
author: Keshab K. Datta
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Visva-Bharati, Santiniketan-731 235, India 

  Fax : 91-3463-262672 

  Manuscript received 4 July 2003. revised 9 March 2004, accepted 11 May 2004  

 A selective method has been developed for extraction chromatographic studies of Al III  with  n -capric acid (liquid cation exchanger) coated on silica gel as stationary phase. Quantitative extraction of Al lll  has been achieved at pH 3.5-5.5. The effect of different acids with varying concentrations, stripping agents, flow rate on extraction and elution have been investigated. Al III  has been separated from binary, ternary and many other multicomponent mixtures as well as from ores and alloy samples.
